title: BEAI Knowledge Loop Scenario Reinforcement
source_reference: Telegram direct messages #4806-#4814 and BEAI Knowledge Loop scenario reinforcement files
query_examples: 왜 아직 자동화나 릴리스로 가지 않았지?
수동 제품 후보 루프라는 판단 근거는?
summary: The user asked to reinforce BEAI Knowledge Loop through scenario simulation rather than stop at a partial usefulness test. The reinforcement added Korean user-language retrieval, stronger companion brief sections, non-record helper counting, regenerated artifacts, and a repeated scenario check that raised the manual usefulness judgment from 6.6 to 8.4.
observed_facts: The original scenario usefulness test scored 6.6 out of 10 and exposed Korean retrieval, brief actionability, and skipped_count weaknesses.
The reinforcement added user-language aliases, query examples, Korean terms, companion brief sections, and non_record_count separation.
The repeated simulation passed 7 out of 7 Korean user-language retrieval scenarios.
user_decisions: The assistant should proceed through reinforcement and scenario simulation directly instead of only explaining the test.
Progress reports should include the number of steps remaining until the current final checkpoint.
The loop should prove repeated usefulness before being considered for automation or release.
assistant_judgments: The correct next move after a 6.6 usefulness score was to strengthen the manual usefulness layer, not to add live automation.
Korean user-language retrieval is a product requirement because the user will naturally ask in Korean, not only in internal English terms.
A manual usefulness score around 8.4 supports continued real-record trials but does not prove automation readiness.
inferred_patterns: A Knowledge Loop record becomes more reusable when it preserves the user's natural-language retrieval hooks alongside structured output classes.
Companion briefs need user-facing decision, definition, risk, evidence, next action, and not-yet-scope sections to help the user make the next judgment.
traps: Treating a passing simulation as release readiness.
Hiding important product boundaries inside internal JSON fields instead of surfacing them in the companion brief.
Counting generated helper files as skipped records and confusing the user about source-record quality.

knowledge_candidates.text: User-language retrieval terms are part of the product surface, not a convenience field.
Manual usefulness should be validated by scenario questions that resemble how the user will actually ask later.

execution_assets.text: Before automating BEAI Knowledge Loop, run at least two more real records through distill, companion brief, index, and Korean retrieval scenarios.

development_candidates.text: Add a repeat-trial verification report that separates manual candidate usefulness from automation readiness.

next_actions: Generate distill JSON and markdown for this reinforcement source record.
Generate a companion brief and confirm reinforced sections remain present.
Regenerate retrieval index and include the record in repeat-trial validation.
rejected_scope: Do not turn the 8.4 usefulness score into release readiness.
Do not create cron, hooks, agents, external connectors, durable memory writes, or release packaging in this repeat-trial step.
next_safe_action: Use this reinforcement record as a repeated real-record trial before considering any live automation or release step.
